title: dryopteris filix-mas var. cochleata
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas var. cochleata, also known as the autumn fern or twisted oak fern, is a variety of the Dryopteris filix-mas plant, a type of deciduous fern commonly found in shaded areas. The plant's rhizomes and leaves are used in herbal medicine, where they are valued for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris filix-mas var. cochleata are believed to help reduce swelling and ease digestive issues, offering health benefits such as soothing digestive problems and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also dried and used as a powder or in capsules for its medicinal properties.
